rulings.Case Title: NATALIE BUFORD et al VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (B/P)AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Leave To File Cross-Complaint
rulings.Rulings: On calendar for Tuesday, March 9, 2010 in Department 220 at 9:30 a.m., Line 1. Defendant Marc Paul, Inc.¿s motion for leave to file cross-complaint is granted. No opposition filed. =(220/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: JOANNE SETTERBERG et al VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (B*P) AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Rulings: On calendar for Tuesday, March 9, 2010 in Department 220 at 9:30 a.m., Line 4. Defendant Metalclad Insulation Corporation¿s motion for summary judgment is denied. Metalclad has failed to establish that the Navy approved reasonably precise specifications regarding the content of warnings for the Unibestos supplied by Metalclad Metalclad¿s alternate motion for summary adjudication is granted in part and denied in part. Summary adjudication as to issue 1 (general negligence), issue 2 (negligent failure to warn) issue 4 (strict liability for failure to warn), and issue 7 (loss of consortium) is denied because Metalclad has failed to establish that the Navy approved reasonably precise specifications regarding the content of warnings for the Unibestos supplied by Metalclad. Summary adjudication as to issue 3 (strict liability for design and manufacturing defect) is granted because Metalclad¿s un-contradicted evidence establishes as a matter of law that the Navy approved reasonably precise specifications regarding the design of the insulation to be supplied by Metalclad, that the specifications were followed and that the Navy had superior knowledge of the hazards of asbestos. Summary adjudication as to issue 8 (punitive damages) is granted because defendant has satisfied its burden of production and plaintiff¿s evidence would not allow a reasonable trier of fact to find by clear and convincing evidence that Metalclad acted with malice, fraud or oppression. Summary adjudication as to issue 5 (false representation) and issue 6 (premises owner/contractor liability) is moot as those claims have been dismissed. =(220/HEK)
